content.full_text:
107 Windsor Palms Dr.
Windsor, CA 95492
September 28, 1992

SEP 30 1992 AM '92

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

re: Request for Advice Letter: Potential Conflict of Interest

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

As per my conversation with Luisa Menchaca of your office on September
18th, I am writing to request a determination as to whether a conflict of
interest exists in the following case. Because there is a time element to be
considered, I am faxing you this letter today, and am mailing a copy to you
as well.

As I informed Ms. Menchaca, I am currently employed as a computer
programmer/analyst at a mortgage banking company, North American
Mortgage Co. (NAMC). I also serve as a member of the Windsor Town
Council. NAMC has 42 production offices in 7 states, and is listed on the
NYSE. NAMC has originated loans in Windsor in the past, and will likely do
so again in the future. The specific projects cannot be determined at this
time. My position is salaried. I receive no commission on loans made by
NAMC, and I am not consulted in decisions regarding loan applications.
Neither my job nor my income is impacted by subdivision approvals or
denials.

NAMC originates, sells, and services loans. NAMC receives approximately
1.5% points and fees at loan origination, approximately 1.5% if it sells a loan,
and a percentage if it services a loan for an investor. All of these are
subject to overhead and cost of services. Mortgage payments are handled
by NAMC.

I own a number of shares in NAMC. The company makes available shares
of stock at a discounted rate to its employees. If this ownership is a
potential conflict of interest, I would be willing to divest myself of them.

I would like an opinion on the following:

1: Can I vote on subdivision approvals? If yes, is there a limitation on the
size subdivision? At what or all stages of approval? Is a phase approval of
a subdivision considered by itself, or as part of the entire subdivision which
might be spread over several years in several phases?

2: Can I vote on an upcoming building permit allocation ordinance, all or in part, that provides for permits for some properties with building permits not honored due to sewer moratorium, properties with time limits, and other projects with approved tentative maps. The ordinance will also limit new applications except for commercial development and affordable housing. Please see the attached ordinance as the probable ordinance to be considered. I have also enclosed a prospectus from NAMC for your information.

If you need additional information, please do not hesitate to contact either me or our Town Attorney, Steven Meyers of Meyers, Nave, Riback, and Silver. I have asked him to contact you separately, as well.

Please expedite your advice letter, as the proposed ordinance will be considered by the Council at our October 28 meeting. I would appreciate having a determination by then. Thank you.

Barbara Siegler
Councilmember,
Town of Windsor
Bus. (707)523-5060
Res. (707)838-2834
FAX (707)546-4020

I have enclosed the prospectus for NAMC. Thank you. Chief.

ORDINANCE NO. 4509

ORDINANCE OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE COUNTY OF SONOMA,
STATE OF CALIFORNIA, MODIFYING AND CONTINUING AN INTERIM
ALLOCATION SYSTEM FOR THE ISSUANCE OF BUILDING PERMITS AND A
SUBDIVISION MORATORIUM IN THAT AREA OF THE COUNTY INCLUDED
WITHIN THE ULTIMATE URBAN SERVICE AREA OF THE WINDSOR SPECIFIC
PLAN

(URGENCY ORDINANCE - 4/5 VOTE REQUIRED)

The Board of Supervisors of the County of Sonoma,
State of California, ordains as follows:

SECTION 1. The Board hereby makes the following
findings:

A. On February 11, 1992, the Board extended the
interim allocation system for the issuance of building permits
in the Windsor Urban Service Area to March 15, 1992, by
Ordinance No. 4500. Ordinance No. 4499 extended the subdivision
moratorium in Windsor. Those ordinances contained historical
findings regarding past and present infrastructure problems in
Windsor. Those findings are incorporated herein by reference.

B. By recent correspondence generated by the
Windsor Water District and reviewed by the State Water Board, it
now appears that there is sewage capacity available and on line
to serve some additional development in Windsor.

C. Residential development which may be served
by the additional sewage capacity are attached hereto and
incorporated herein by reference as Exhibit "A-1" and "A-2."

D. Although additional sewage capacity exists,
nonsewage related infrastructure problems still exist in
Windsor. Moreover, sewage demands placed by industrial,
commercial and institutional development in Windsor will reduce
the new sewage capacity which has just come on line. Because of
these factors, it is necessary to establish an allocation
system to continue to regulate the pace at which building permits are
issued in Windsor. The purpose of this ordinance is to
establish a fair and equitable allocation system for residential
development. No regulation is being placed on commercial,
industrial and institutional land uses because of the need to
bolster the tax base of the City of Windsor which is due to
incorporate on July 1, 1992.

E. Without this allocation system, there still
remains an immediate threat to the public peace, health and
safety.

SKB749                                1                SKB:dlb/03-03-92

C          expire on March 15, 1992. This ordinance will take effect
immediately and supercedes Ordinance Nos. 4499 and 4500.

SECTION II.

A. No new application for a building permit may
be received or processed within the ultimate urban service area
of the Windsor Specific Plan as delineated on that map attached
hereto as Exhibit "B" which is incorporated herein by
reference.

B. No building permits shall be issued within
the ultimate urban service area of the Windsor Specific Plan for
which there is currently pending an application on file with the
County.

C. Notwithstanding the limits set forth in
Sections II A and II B above, building permits for construction
which will generate no more than 1337 ESDs may be accepted,
processed and issued in accordance with the building permit
allocation system established in Section III of this ordinance.

SECTION III.

Building permits generating no more than 1337 ESDs may
be accepted, processed and issued in accordance with the
following building permit allocation system:

A. Building permits for industrial, commercial
and institutional land uses are exempt from the prohibitions of
Section II and the allocation system set forth in this Section
III.

B. Building permits for multi-family projects
and for deed restricted low, very low and moderate income
residential units are exempt from the prohibitions of Section II
and the allocation system set forth in this Section III in order
to encourage the construction of affordable housing.

C. Building permits for single family dwellings
on lots created through subdivisions of four units or less are
exempt from the prohibitions of Section II and the allocation
system set forth in this Section III.

D. Existing residential lots not shown on
Exhibit A-2 are exempt from the prohibitions of Section II and
the allocation system set forth in this Section III.

E. Residential subdivisions as set forth in
Exhibit "A-1" are exempt from the prohibitions of Section II and
the allocation system set forth in this Section III.


SKB749                    2                    SKB:dlb/03-03-92

obtain building permits in accordance with the yearly phasing
schedule set forth in Exhibit "A-2."

G. Applications for subdivisions of four or
fewer units, whether major or minor, may be accepted and
processed. No other subdivision applications will be received
until after July 1, 1992, except an application consisting of
all low, very low or moderate income housing units.

H. Building permits allocated but not used in
one calendar year can be used in the following years.

I. After notice to the County, building permits
which are allocated to a single integrated development may be
transferred to a different phase of the same development
provided that both phases are owned by the same owner. No
County consent is needed for.this type of transfer.

J. Building permit allocations may be
transferred between different ownerships with the prior written
consent of the County as evidenced by a signed letter from the
Planning Director.

K. Building permit allocations are not
entitlements or property rights. This ordinance may be changed
to revise the allocation system at any time in the interests of
health, safety or general welfare concerns. No vested right
accrues until a residential building permit has been issued and
significant construction commenced on the affected lot.

L. Quarterly reports shall be provided to the
Board of Supervisors and to the Windsor Town Council. The
Planning Department will provide the Windsor Town Council with a
status report on July 1, 1992.

M. This ordinance shall constitute a moratorium
as that term is defined and applied by Government Code section
66452.6(b) and (f).

N. For purposes of this ordinance, moderate
income housing units are those which are deed restricted to
families with income not exceeding 100 percent of the median
income as published by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban
Development for Sonoma County.

SECTION IV. The temporary restrictions on the
issuance of building permits and the allocation system set forth
herein shall continue until July 1, 1992, at which time it may
be extended by a vote of the Windsor City Council.

SECTION V. If any section, subsection, sentence,
clause or phrase of this ordinance is for any reason held to be
unconstitutional and invalid, such decision shall not affect the

SKB749                3                SKB:dlb/03-03-92

validity of the remaining portion of this ordinance. The Board
of Supervisors hereby declares that it would have passed this
ordinance and every section, subsection, sentence, clause or
phrase thereof, irrespective of the fact that any one or more
sections, subsections, sentences, clauses or phrases be declared
unconstitutional or invalid.

SECTION VI. This ordinance shall be and the same is
hereby declared to be in full force immediately and shall be
published once before the expiration of fifteen (15) days after
said passage, with the names of the Supervisors voting for or
against the same, in The Press Democrat, a newspaper of general
circulation published in the County of Sonoma, State of California.

In regular session of the Board of Supervisors of the
County of Sonoma, State of California, introduced and adopted this
ordinance on an urgency basis on this 3rd day of March
1992, on regular roll call of the members of said Board by the
following vote:

SUPERVISORS:

CALE_____HARBERSON_____ESPOSITI_____CARPENTER_____SMITH_____

AYES_5___NOES_____ABSTAIN_____ABSENT_____


WHEREUPON, the Chair declared the above and foregoing
ordinance duly adopted and

SO ORDERED.

_________________________
Chair, Board of Supervisors
County of Sonoma

ATTEST:

_________________________
EEVE T. LEWIS, County Clerk and
ex-officio Clerk of the Board of
Supervisors


SKB749                                    4                    SKB:dlb/03-03-92
